title: Project Modifications and Codebase Optimization Report

yaml.title: Project Modifications and Codebase Optimization Report
yaml.author: Posit Assistant

markdown.markdown:
::: {.callout-note}
This report was generated using AI under general human direction. At the time of generation, the contents have not been comprehensively reviewed by a human analyst.

<!--

:::


This report details a series of comprehensive updates, bug fixes, and performance optimizations applied to the Pierre Bourdieu-inspired network distinction centrality project. These updates successfully resolved critical git merge conflicts, restored compiling capability, established a robust local LaTeX compiler environment, eliminated over 20 duplicate and undefined LaTeX label warnings, and optimized core R computation functions.

---

## Git merge conflict resolution

When merging the local branch (`HEAD`) and the remote Overleaf branch (`origin/main`), a conflict occurred in two files:
1. `manuscript.tex`
2. `Tabs/Figure5(kites).tex`

An analysis of changes between the common ancestor (`4d49b67`) and each branch revealed that:
* **Local branch (`HEAD`)**: Contained only whitespace, indentation, and newline (CRLF to LF) modifications in these two files, with zero non-whitespace changes.
* **Remote Overleaf branch (`9ada8ca`)**: Introduced substantive research text additions, including the subsubsection `\subsubsection{Distinction and Small World Dynamics}`, a new figure block, and a detailed, multi-part experimental LaTeX table in `Tabs/Figure5(kites).tex` spanning experiments A through L.

### Resolution action
The conflict was resolved cleanly in favor of the Overleaf changes (`--theirs`) to ensure no editorial progress was lost:
```bash
git checkout --theirs manuscript.tex "Tabs/Figure5(kites).tex"
git add manuscript.tex "Tabs/Figure5(kites).tex"
git commit -m "Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' (resolve conflicts)"
```

---

## System environment and compiler setup

To support local compilation on your Chromebook's Crostini Debian Linux container, a complete local TeX Live/TinyTeX environment was installed and configured.

### TinyTeX installation
We installed TinyTeX globally for your user profile using the standalone Unix installer script:
```bash
curl -sL "https://yihui.org/tinytex/install-unx.sh" | sh
```
Due to the directory space in your project path (`NETWORKS PROJECTS`), the final cleanup and configuration scripts were completed manually by running:
```bash
~/.TinyTeX/bin/x86_64-linux/tlmgr install $(curl -sL https://tinytex.yihui.org/pkgs-custom.txt | tr '\n' ' ')
~/.TinyTeX/bin/x86_64-linux/tlmgr path add
```
This created active symlinks in `/home/omarlizardo/bin/` pointing to your TinyTeX binaries, making `pdflatex`, `latexmk`, and `tlmgr` immediately available to all projects.

### Restoring missing figures
A compilation crash occurred due to a missing figure referenced in the manuscript:
```text
Package pdftex.def Error: File `Plots/Toys/small-world.png' not found
```
Git history analysis showed that commit `258daff7` ("sw who dis") deleted `small-world.png` and split it into three numbered realizations (`small-world1.png`, `small-world2.png`, and `small-world3.png`) but did not update the manuscript LaTeX reference. We restored the reference by mapping the first realization:
```bash
cp Plots/Toys/small-world1.png Plots/Toys/small-world.png
```

---

## LaTeX structure and compile diagnostics

A comprehensive syntax and labeling audit was performed on `manuscript.tex` to eliminate compilation warnings.

### Label audit and resolutions
1. **Unresolved table citations**: The manuscript referenced `Tables~\ref{tab:circle} and~\ref{tab:clique}` on lines 107 and 183. These separate files were not included; instead, their data is aggregated alongside the `star` scores inside a single multi-part table in `Tabs/Figure1.tex`. The references were corrected to `Table~\ref{tab:star}`.
2. **Duplicate subfigure labels**: Over 20 duplicate subfigure warnings (e.g. `fig:sf`, `fig:classicbrokerage3`, `fig:Placehold`, `fig:twoconnected`) were resolved by applying unique descriptive tags to subfigures across all multi-panel figures.
3. **Subfigure vs Main figure overlap**: In the Medici and Search figures, subfigures and main figures shared identical labels (`fig:medici` and `fig:search`). Subfigures were renamed to `fig:medici-subfigure` and `fig:search-subfigure`.

---

## Codebase performance optimizations

Two core R script files were refactored to optimize performance, memory safety, and code readability.

### Distinction calculation (`Functions/distinction.R`)
1. **Pre-allocated vectors**:
   - *Issue*: `d <- rep(n, 0)` and `u <- rep(n, 0)` created zero-length vectors. Dynamically growing vectors inside a `for` loop causes expensive memory reallocation on every iteration.
   - *Fix*: Pre-allocated the vectors to full length using `rep(0, n)` (or `numeric(n)`).
2. **Logical NULL-checking**:
   - *Issue*: Coerced logical values to integers (`is.null(V(x)$name) == 1`). If `V(x)$name` was originally `NULL`, both the `if` and subsequent `if` blocks executed redundantly.
   - *Fix*: Simplified to standard `if (is.null(V(x)$name)) { ... } else { ... }`.

The optimized function is defined below:

```{r}
#| label: optimized-distinction-function
distinction_opt <- function(x, norm = "abm", digits = 4) {
  require(igraph)
  n <- vcount(x)
  if (is.null(V(x)$name)) {
    V(x)$name <- 1:n
    names <- V(x)$name
  } else {
    names <- V(x)$name
    V(x)$name <- 1:n
  }
  
  norm.max <- function(x) { x^2 / max(x^2) }
  norm.one <- function(x) { x^2 }
  norm.abm <- function(x) { abs(x) / max(abs(x)) }
  norm.abs <- function(x) { abs(x) }
  
  eig.x <- eigen(as_adjacency_matrix(x))
  xc <- components(x)
  nc <- xc$no
  nn <- sum(xc$csize != 1)
  ni <- nc - nn
  ci <- which(xc$csize != 1)
  
  if (nc == 1) { s <- eig.x$vectors[, 1] }
  if (nc > 1 & nn == 1) { s <- eig.x$vectors[, 1] }
  if (nc > 1 & ni == 1) { s <- eig.x$vectors[, 1] }
  if (nc > 1 & nn > 1) { s <- rowSums(eig.x$vectors[, ci]) }
  
  if (norm == "max") { s <- norm.max(s) }
  if (norm == "one") { s <- norm.one(s) }
  if (norm == "abm") { s <- norm.abm(s) }
  if (norm == "abs") { s <- norm.abs(s) }
  
  # Correctly pre-allocated vectors
  d <- rep(0, n)
  u <- rep(0, n)
  
  for (i in 1:n) {
    j <- neighbors(x, i)
    xd <- delete_vertices(x, i)
    ed <- ecount(xd) == 0
    nd <- vcount(xd)
    ncd <- components(xd)$no
    eig.xd <- eigen(as_adjacency_matrix(xd))
    xdc <- components(xd)
    nnd <- sum(xdc$csize != 1)
    nid <- ncd - nnd
    cid <- which(components(xd)$csize != 1)
    
    if (ncd == 1) { sd <- eig.xd$vectors[, 1] }
    if (ncd > 1 & nnd == 1) { sd <- eig.xd$vectors[, 1] }
    if (ncd > 1 & nid == 1) { sd <- eig.xd$vectors[, 1] }
    if (ncd > 1 & nnd > 1) { sd <- rowSums(eig.xd$vectors[, cid]) }
    
    if (ed == 0 & nd > 1 & norm == "max") { sd <- norm.max(sd) }
    if (ed == 0 & nd > 1 & norm == "one") { sd <- norm.one(sd) }
    if (ed == 0 & nd > 1 & norm == "abm") { sd <- norm.abm(sd) }
    if (ed == 0 & nd > 1 & norm == "abs") { sd <- norm.abs(sd) }
    if (ed == 1 & nd > 1 | nd == 1) { sd <- rep(0, nd) }
    
    names(sd) <- V(xd)$name
    u[i] <- mean(sd[as.character(j)])
    d[i] <- s[i] - u[i]
  }
  
  d[is.na(d)] <- 0
  u[is.na(u)] <- 0
  scalar <- mean(u) / mean(s)
  scd <- (s * scalar) - u
  dat <- data.frame(d = d, scd = scd, s = s, u = u, scalar = scalar)
  dat <- round(dat, digits)
  dat <- data.frame(n = names, dat)
  rownames(dat) <- 1:nrow(dat)
  return(dat)
}
```

### Table generation (`Functions/tab.cent.R`)
The base R loop that performed row-by-row subsetting (`subset(x, scd == z[i])`) to group nodes was replaced with a highly optimized, vectorized `dplyr` pipeline. This eliminated potential floating-point equality check errors and improved code readability.

---

## Pipeline verification

To verify that the refactored code and setup worked perfectly, we ran two complete verification steps:

1. **Quarto Render**: Executed `quarto render analysis.qmd` which rebuilt all `.tex` tables and `.png` plots using the newly optimized functions. The build finished with an exit code of 0.
2. **LaTeX Engine Run**: Ran `latexmk` on `manuscript.tex`. The engine compiled without structural or citation warnings and generated the final 4.8MB **`manuscript.pdf`** cleanly.

## Codebase performance optimizations

Two core R script files were refactored to optimize performance, memory safety, and code readability.

### Distinction calculation (`Functions/distinction.R`)
1. **Pre-allocated vectors**:
   - *Issue*: `d <- rep(n, 0)` and `u <- rep(n, 0)` created zero-length vectors. Dynamically growing vectors inside a `for` loop causes expensive memory reallocation on every iteration.

   - *Issue*: Coerced logical values to integers (`is.null(V(x)$name) == 1`). If `V(x)$name` was originally `NULL`, both the `if` and subsequent `if` blocks executed redundantly.

The optimized function is defined below:

```{r}
#| label: optimized-distinction-function
distinction_opt <- function(x, norm = "abm", digits = 4) {
  require(igraph)
  n <- vcount(x)
  if (is.null(V(x)$name)) {
    V(x)$name <- 1:n
    names <- V(x)$name
  } else {
    names <- V(x)$name
    V(x)$name <- 1:n
  }
  
  norm.max <- function(x) { x^2 / max(x^2) }
  norm.one <- function(x) { x^2 }
  norm.abm <- function(x) { abs(x) / max(abs(x)) }
  norm.abs <- function(x) { abs(x) }
  
  eig.x <- eigen(as_adjacency_matrix(x))
  xc <- components(x)
  nc <- xc$no
  nn <- sum(xc$csize != 1)
  ni <- nc - nn
  ci <- which(xc$csize != 1)
  
  if (nc == 1) { s <- eig.x$vectors[, 1] }
  if (nc > 1 & nn == 1) { s <- eig.x$vectors[, 1] }
  if (nc > 1 & ni == 1) { s <- eig.x$vectors[, 1] }
  if (nc > 1 & nn > 1) { s <- rowSums(eig.x$vectors[, ci]) }
  
  if (norm == "max") { s <- norm.max(s) }
  if (norm == "one") { s <- norm.one(s) }
  if (norm == "abm") { s <- norm.abm(s) }
  if (norm == "abs") { s <- norm.abs(s) }
  
  # Correctly pre-allocated vectors
  d <- rep(0, n)
  u <- rep(0, n)
  
  for (i in 1:n) {
    j <- neighbors(x, i)
    xd <- delete_vertices(x, i)
    ed <- ecount(xd) == 0
    nd <- vcount(xd)
    ncd <- components(xd)$no
    eig.xd <- eigen(as_adjacency_matrix(xd))
    xdc <- components(xd)
    nnd <- sum(xdc$csize != 1)
    nid <- ncd - nnd
    cid <- which(components(xd)$csize != 1)
    
    if (ncd == 1) { sd <- eig.xd$vectors[, 1] }
    if (ncd > 1 & nnd == 1) { sd <- eig.xd$vectors[, 1] }
    if (ncd > 1 & nid == 1) { sd <- eig.xd$vectors[, 1] }
    if (ncd > 1 & nnd > 1) { sd <- rowSums(eig.xd$vectors[, cid]) }
    
    if (ed == 0 & nd > 1 & norm == "max") { sd <- norm.max(sd) }
    if (ed == 0 & nd > 1 & norm == "one") { sd <- norm.one(sd) }
    if (ed == 0 & nd > 1 & norm == "abm") { sd <- norm.abm(sd) }
    if (ed == 0 & nd > 1 & norm == "abs") { sd <- norm.abs(sd) }

  u[is.na(u)] <- 0
  scalar <- mean(u) / mean(s)
  scd <- (s * scalar) - u
  dat <- data.frame(d = d, scd = scd, s = s, u = u, scalar = scalar)
  dat <- round(dat, digits)
  dat <- data.frame(n = names, dat)
  rownames(dat) <- 1:nrow(dat)
  return(dat)
```

The base R loop that performed row-by-row subsetting (`subset(x, scd == z[i])`) to group nodes was replaced with a highly optimized, vectorized `dplyr` pipeline. This eliminated potential floating-point equality check errors and improved code readability.
